channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This travel site is the Google Maps helper you never knew you needed
item.description: Trying to get from point A to point B? If only it were that simple! With any manner of travel these days, you’ve got options: planes, trains, buses, ferries, and beyond. And finding the best path to embark on isn’t always easy. Even finding all the available options can sometimes be a pain.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: Senate Democrat presses Noem on Real ID deadline
item.description: Sen. Jack Reed (D-R.I.) pressed Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on the Real ID requirements being implemented in two weeks. The new measure requires individuals to provide a U.S. birth certificate or a U.S. passport, or a permanent resident card (green card) if they are a non-U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 pre-orders will start in the United States on April 24, and the price is not increasing
item.description: Nintendo has finally announced that Switch 2 preorders will start in the United States on April 24, 2025 with no price increase for the console.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Federal judge rules Google has illegal ad tech monopoly
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that Google illegally acquired and maintained a monopoly over advertising technology, the second time in less than a year that the tech giant has been found in violation of antitrust laws. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Many Americans share mistrust of artificial intelligence: Survey
item.description: Three-quarters of Americans say they trust information generated by artificial intelligence (AI) only sometimes or hardly ever, according to a recent survey.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: GE Vernova’s CEO on thriving through tariffs and supply chain shifts
item.description: Amid tariff whiplash and the rejuggling of global trade, GE Vernova’s CEO Scott Strazik is finding a way to stay “relentlessly optimistic.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Zuckerberg underscores TikTok competition as Meta fights monopoly allegations
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized Wednesday that his social media company faces stiff competition from TikTok, as Meta seeks to fend off accusations from the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) that it has a monopoly over social networking.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Intel sells off majority stake in its FPGA business
item.description: Intel has spun off its programmable solutions group as a standalone FPGA company, selling a majority stake in the company to a private equity firm. Intel is taking a fairly hefty loss on this deal.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Analysts: Go slow on M365, Google Workspace ‘agent-ish’ AI rollouts
item.description: The last few months have seen an explosion of new agentic-like AI tools that can automate workflows within productivity applications, but analysts are recommending that enterprises carefully assess deployment risks before adopting them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft at 50: The 7 biggest game-changers through five decades
item.description: When Microsoft was founded 50 years ago this month, it wasn’t clear the company would last 50 weeks, much less 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration
item.description: A key focus will be on modernizing federal technology and software to maximize governmental efficiency and productivity. The post The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Why digital transformation starts with an intelligent network infrastructure
item.description: Heightened end-user expectations and a greater emphasis on digitalization are rewiring the business landscape again.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: White House launches national security investigation into pharma, semiconductors
item.description: The Trump administration disclosed Monday that it had opened an investigation into the effects on national security of importing certain pharmaceuticals, a move widely seen as the prelude to initiating tariffs on prescription drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia lays out plans to build AI supercomputers in the US
item.description: There was mixed reaction from industry analysts Monday over an announcement from Nvidia that it plans to produce AI supercomputers entirely in the US for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Windows Server 2025 domain controllers may lose connectivity after reboot, says Microsoft
item.description: Microsoft is warning IT administrators that their Windows Server 2025 domain controllers (DC) may lose network connectivity after they are restarted.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DeepSeek’s open source movement
item.description: DeepSeek may have originated in China, but it stopped being Chinese the minute it was released on Hugging Face with an accompanying paper detailing its development.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How pet projects fuel innovation and careers in tech
item.description: Call them pet projects, side projects, or hobby projects, projects pursued outside of one’s regular business activity are especially popular among software developers. After all, these projects serve as an excellent way to learn new skills and technologies or to test fresh concepts and approaches.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 7 reasons low-code and no-code tools fail to deliver
item.description: The potential benefits of low-code and no-code development tools include faster application development, lower expenses, and more agility. The technology is not suited to every scenario, however, and in some cases, low- and no-code solutions could be a barrier to productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta vs. Federal Trade Commission trial begins Monday. What to know
item.description: The FTC alleges Meta violated competition laws when purchasing Instagram and WhatsApp, in 2012 and 2014 respectively.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, ABC 4
